reviews.review_description: I kept passing this one up for other games.  I then tried it and did not stop until I beat it.  I have not done all the hidden doors, some of those are harder.  I think level 23... the one where you basically are going straight up...woah, that was intense and I had to hold on to something while doing that level...so high up.  The gameplay is just fun, swinging and flying, slapping your sticky hand to propel yourself farther.  My only complaint is that the moving cloud levels are very hard to determine what is going on.  I hope you guys plan on making more.  Very fun game.

I have all types of Go apps: adventure games like Along Together and Virtual Virtual Reality; action games like Void Racer and Endspace. But SwingStar might be the most fun. It’s the closest to a Nintendo platformer for the Go that I’ve played. Difficulty increases gradually and stages become quite complex. On some levels, there are secret areas with hidden keys that unlock doors to bonus levels.

The act of flinging yourself off blocks with your flexible arm is immediately fun. As you go, stages become more vast, allowing you to soar to incredible heights and free-fall back down. Stages that encourage extra exploration are especially fun, and most stages allow multiple options to reach your goal. At the end of the game, there’s a spectacular boss fight — one of the better experiences I’ve had in VR games. It’s a game you want to keep playing to get a better score, to get to the next level, and just because it’s fun.

Sometimes, especially at high speed, your hand doesn’t grab the block even when you *know* you clicked on it, which can be frustrating. But it doesn’t happen often enough to put much of a damper on things. 

Overall, it’s one of the most underrated, under-appreciated games on the Go. I’m glad I found it.
Although the next levels are mia right now this current short game is outstanding and worth it
With 16 levels, which includes 4-5 tutorial levels, the length is more appropriate for a demo than a full game.

developer_response.body: Check out the new update, we added a bunch of new levels, new environments, a  boss battle, and secret doors to unlock.

reviews.review_description: Great game that's a good change from all the lame coaster and "scary" games and apps on Oculus. I admit that it had issues loading and playing on my S7 and Gear VR headset, but after an update today, it works great! Thanks for this great game!

developer_response.body: Thanks! Glad we help fix the loading issue.

reviews.review_description: While most titles for the gear VR focus on immersion, this one balances that with the fun of a good old classic NES Style game. Although the first 2/3 of the levels are not really very challenging, they're still fun. Overall, this is just a fun game to play and well worth the price. I feel like it's a little under promoted given how different it is from all of the other Oculus titles, and I think it would be hard not to like it.

A fun mechanic combined with a varied selection of levels make for an enjoyable if all too short experience. The 3D stereoscopy effect is sadly practically turned off, no doubt to preempt nausea in some players. It would be better to give the players a comfort vs. intensity option in the menus. I personally enjoy getting dizzy. Maybe in future updates?

developer_response.body: Hey! The stereoscopy is now back in. We actually didn't notice it was missing. Thank you for alerting us of this.

reviews.review_description: Easy to play.  Cute character, fun and not to hard or to easy to play.

developer_response.body: Glad your family liked it! Check out the update form more levels and a boss battle.

reviews.review_description: Short but fun

developer_response.body: We added 15 levels since released now new power up items and a giant robot boss battle at the end.

reviews.review_description: Much fun. The jelly power ups are the best. Highly recommended.

reviews.review_title: Hat swinging starts crappy but get fun
reviews.review_description: Use your hat to swing box to box starts boring and simple but gets much much better after a few stages you try to get 3 stars on each stage complete the stage by getting too the bell in a minimal amount of swings its a fun simple one button game its priced right at $2.99 simple but fun
Doesn't work anymore
This awsome game is fun go to youtube watch the video with a chance to win a free copy of this wonderfull game
This swinging platformer has the feel of Mario 64, in levels, and the gameplay of spiderma,n swinging from block to block! If that sounds good to you and you have $3 to spend I'd recommend this game.
Good plain and simple fun.
Great game with a fun swinging mechanic. You will laugh when you mess up and immediately want to try again.
I love this game. It’s so much fun. I  wish they had it for the quest 2
Would be so much better in first person mode

developer_response.body: We did try a first person mode but it made people feel nauseous. We instead use a third person camera design to help prevents motion sickness by giving players a object to focus on while they swing though the air.

reviews.review_title: Fun game, but way too short
reviews.review_description: Loved the game, but I managed to beat it in a half hour. If there was a lot more levels, itd definitely be worth the $2.99 though.

developer_response.body: Hi papercutz, try the SwingStar update it's way longer, we added more levels, a boss battle, and secret doors to unlock.
